title: Unified Configuration Document
description: The single schema-validated document where operators change platform configuration (TR-17, Batch 16 Task 2). `config` holds unified keys grouped by domain; `bindings` maps every unified key to the native configuration location of a wrapped system. Three cross-file rules are VALIDATOR-ENFORCED by scripts/ci/validate_management_plane_contracts.sh and are intentionally NOT restated as schema constraints, because JSON Schema cannot express cross-file references (see docs/DECISIONS.md, Batch 15 entry, on softening prose the schema cannot enforce): (1) every binding's `system` must be a `system` id registered in contracts/management/WRAPPED_SYSTEM_REGISTRY_V1.yaml - bindings targeting unregistered systems are rejected; (2) every leaf key present under `config` must be targeted by at least one binding - unbound keys are rejected; (3) every binding's `unified_key` must resolve to a leaf key present under `config` - bindings to unknown config keys are rejected. Propagation is GitOps-only: every `render_target` is a repository path that is rendered, committed, and reconciled by the GitOps controller - never a live endpoint or mutable API (PROPAGATION_RECONCILIATION_CONTRACT_V1.yaml). Unified keys carry policy values only (retention days, ratios, toggles, channel identities); endpoints, hosts, and credentials are forbidden here and live in per-environment overlays or the secrets adapter. Rejection fixtures: samples/INVALID_UNIFIED_CONFIG_SAMPLES.json. Versioned per contracts/CONTRACTS_NAMING_CONVENTION.md; breaking key or binding changes require a new UNIFIED_CONFIG_SCHEMA_V<N>.json with documented migration notes, never a rename.

schema_version.description: Version marker for the unified configuration document format.

config.description: Unified keys grouped by domain. Domains are optional so partial documents (for example rejection fixtures) stay schema-valid, but the canonical committed document is complete: samples/VALID_UNIFIED_CONFIG.yaml carries every domain. Presence-based completeness (every present leaf key bound) is validator-enforced, not schema-enforced.

retention.description: Per-signal retention policy, propagated to the OpenSearch ILM policies under gitops/platform/search/opensearch/ilm/.

logs_days.description: Days before log indices are deleted (ILM delete transition min_index_age).

metrics_days.description: Days before metrics indices are deleted.

traces_days.description: Days before trace indices are deleted. Traces default shorter than logs/metrics (higher cardinality, shorter operational value).

tracing.description: Trace collection policy for the OpenTelemetry Collector traces pipeline.

sampling_ratio.description: Baseline parentbased_traceidratio sampling ratio (tracesPipeline.sampling.default.ratio). Per-environment overrides stay in environment overlays, not in this document.

tls.description: TLS posture policy for wrapped UIs. Ingress and TLS termination are owned by the access-layer Application and per-environment overlays; this document carries posture policy only, never certificates or hosts.

enforce_secure_cookies.description: Require Secure session cookies on wrapped UIs (for example grafana.ini security.cookie_secure).

alerting.description: Alert notification routing policy for the rendered alerting rules under gitops/platform/search/dashboards/alerts/.

notification_channels.description: Logical channel identities referenced by rendered alert rules. Endpoints, webhook URLs, and credentials are never part of the unified document (no hard-coded environment values); they are resolved through the secrets adapter and per-environment overlays.

name.description: Stable lowercase channel identity referenced from alert rules.

kind.description: Channel transport kind. Provider specifics belong to adapters.

dashboards.description: Dashboard provisioning toggles for the visualization plane.

provisioning_enabled.description: Master toggle for declarative dashboard provisioning: Grafana sidecar dashboard loading and OpenSearch Dashboards saved-object bundle provisioning.

graph.description: Optional derived graph tier enablement (Neo4j is never a raw telemetry store).

enabled.description: Enables the Neo4j graph module and the collector graph pipeline.

browser_access_enabled.description: Publishes Neo4j Browser behind the admin access plane. Effective only when graph.enabled is true; the dependency is validator-enforced territory for the propagation contract, not this schema.

default_isolation_class.description: Isolation class assigned to new tenants when their tenant contract does not set one explicitly.

delivery.description: GitOps delivery behavior for the Argo CD Applications under gitops/apps/.

auto_sync_enabled.description: Whether Application specs render with spec.syncPolicy.automated (prune + selfHeal). Manual-sync posture renders the block absent.

bindings.description: Per-system propagation bindings. Each binding routes one unified key to one native configuration location of one registered wrapped system. A unified key may have multiple bindings (fan-out to several systems); every present key must have at least one (validator-enforced, see top-level description).

unified_key.description: Dotted path of a leaf key under `config` (for example retention.logs_days). Must resolve to a key present in this document's `config` - validator-enforced.

system.description: A `system` id registered in contracts/management/WRAPPED_SYSTEM_REGISTRY_V1.yaml. Registration is validator-enforced; the id set is intentionally not duplicated here as an enum so the registry stays the single source of truth.

native_path.description: The native configuration location the unified key propagates into. repo_path must fall under one of the registered system's config_surface paths in the registry - validator-enforced.

repo_path.description: Repository path of the native artifact (Helm values file, provisioning file, ILM policy, saved-object or alert-rule directory).

locator.description: Location inside repo_path: a Helm values dotted key, a JSON field path in an index template or ILM policy, or a named section of a provisioning artifact.

render_target.description: Repository path the rendered native output lands in, committed and reconciled by the GitOps controller. Always a Git path - live endpoints and mutable API targets are forbidden (GitOps-only propagation).
